--- title: "India's Public Foodgrain Procurement: Trends, Patterns & Policy Implications" description: "A detailed analysis of foodgrain procurement by FCI and state agencies across marketing seasons, with regional breakdowns and MSP evolution." author: "Pawan" date: "2025/10/30" date-modified: last-modified categories: - "Food Economy" - "Procurement" - "Agriculture Policy" format: html: code-fold: true toc: true toc-depth: 3 theme: cosmo editor_options: chunk_output_type: console execute: error: false message: false warning: false eval: true --- ## Overview India operates one of the world's largest public food procurement systems. The government procures wheat (rabi), paddy/rice (kharif), and coarse grains at the **Minimum Support Price (MSP)** through the Food Corporation of India (FCI) and state government agencies. Procured stocks underpin the **Public Distribution System (PDS)**, midday-meal schemes, and the National Food Security Act (NFSA) welfare programmes. **Key policy features:** - Procurement is price-floor insurance for farmers — FCI and state agencies buy all offered quantities at MSP. - Stocks feed welfare: NFSA covers 80 crore beneficiaries with 5 kg/person/month of subsidised grain. - Buffer norms guide minimum stock requirements for food security. --- ## Setup ::: {.cell} ::: --- ## Section 1 · Total Procurement by Crop ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 2 · FCI vs State Agency Split The procurement machinery involves two tiers: FCI (central agency) and state government agencies. Their relative roles have shifted over time — states have taken on more responsibility as part of decentralisation. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 3 · Procurement Trend Analysis ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 4 · Summary Statistics Table ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Procurement Summary Statistics by Crop
Crop Avg (LMT) Max (LMT) Min (LMT) Avg FCI Share % No. of Seasons
Paddy 566.3 913.7 246.6 2.3 19
Wheat 252.8 433.4 92.2 5.3 25
Source: DFPD Central Food Grains Procurement Portal
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 5 · Policy Context ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: > **Key insights:** >1. **Marketing Season** for procurement is different for rabi and kharif crops, For Rabi crops RMS (Wheat) is begin from 1st April and end 31st March, For Kharif crops KMS (Paddy) is begin from 1st October end at 30th September > 2. **Paddy dominates** procurement in volume terms, with Chhattisgarh, MP, Punjab and Haryana as the largest procuring states. > 3. **Wheat procurement** fluctuates more sharply due to production variability in the Indo-Gangetic plain. > 4. **State agencies** have grown as a share of total procurement, reflecting greater state-level operational capacity. > 5. **COVID-19 impact** (2020–21): procurement held firm, underpinning the free-grain scheme (PMGKAY) for 80 crore beneficiaries.
